learning.concept: A programming language is a way to communicate with computers.
learning.explanation: Computers naturally understand only binary (0 and 1). Programming languages like Python or Java allow humans to write instructions in a much more readable way, which is then translated for the computer.

analogies.title: Human Languages
analogies.body: Just like humans use English, Hindi, or Spanish to talk to each other, computers use Python, Java, or C++ to receive commands from us.

analogies.title: A Musical Partition
analogies.body: The language is the notation. One language (Classical) is perfect for a symphony, while another (Tablature) is perfect for guitar. Each language has its own symbols.

analogies.title: Architect's Blueprint
analogies.body: A blueprint uses specific symbols to mean 'Wall' or 'Door'. A programming language uses symbols (+, -, if, print) to mean 'Add', 'Subtract', 'Decide', or 'Show'.

examples.title: High-Level Language
examples.description: Python is like simplified English. It's designed for humans to read and write easily.

examples.title: Low-Level Language
examples.description: Assembly or Machine Code is very close to the 0s and 1s of the CPU hardware.

examples.title: Specialized Language
examples.description: SQL is a language used ONLY for talking to databases, while HTML is for building the structure of websites.

practice.q: Why can’t we talk to computers in English directly?
practice.a: Computers don't understand context or nuances of human language like we do.
Yes, it is one of the most popular languages for learning and professional work.
To give instructions efficiently without having to type in binary code manually.
